perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean all non-public proprietary information provided by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party, whether or not marked or otherwise specifically designated as confidential. The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which (i) becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party, (ii) was available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior the Furnishing Party's disclosure to the Receiving Party, or (iii) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Furnishing Party, provided that such source is, to the best of the Receiving Party's knowledge, not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Furnishing Party or otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to the Furnishing Party by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean all non-public proprietary information provided by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party, whether or not marked or otherwise specifically designated as confidential. The term "Confidential Information" also includes information which (i) becomes generally available to the public as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party, (ii) was available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior the Furnishing Party's disclosure to the Receiving Party, or (iii) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Furnishing Party, provided that such source is, to the best of the Receiving Party's knowledge, not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Furnishing Party or otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to the Furnishing Party by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'does not include' to 'also includes', the definition of 'Confidential Information' now explicitly contradicts itself. The original clause excludes publicly available information. The modified clause includes it, creating an in-text contradiction regarding what constitutes 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" shall mean all non-public proprietary information provided by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party, whether or not marked or otherwise specifically designated as confidential. The term "Confidential Information" also includes information which (i) becomes generally available to the public as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party, (ii) was available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior the Furnishing Party's disclosure to the Receiving Party, or (iii) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Furnishing Party, provided that such source is, to the best of the Receiving Party's knowledge, not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Furnishing Party or otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to the Furnishing Party by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by changing 'does not include' to 'also includes'. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text explicitly excludes certain information (like publicly available information) from being classified as confidential, while the changed text includes it. This contradicts the fundamental understanding of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' in the document, as evidenced by the contradicted text which has the same contradicting clause. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists, meeting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. It is understood that the Receiving Party may disclose any of the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party's (and its subsidiaries' and affiliates') directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys and financial advisors), and prospective bank or institutional lenders, (collectively, "Representatives") who require such material in connection with a possible or actual Transaction (provided that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall agree to act in accordance with the terms hereof). The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information of the Furnishing Party will be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and its Representatives and, except with the specific prior written consent of the Furnishing Party, or as expressly otherwise permitted by the terms hereof, will not be disclosed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives. The Parties further agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than in connection with a possible or actual Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. It is understood that the Receiving Party may disclose any of the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party's (and its subsidiaries' and affiliates') directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys and financial advisors), and prospective bank or institutional lenders, (collectively, "Representatives") who require such material in connection with a possible or actual Transaction (provided that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall agree to act in accordance with the terms hereof). The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information of the Furnishing Party may be disclosed by the Receiving Party and its Representatives and, except with the specific prior written consent of the Furnishing Party, or as expressly otherwise permitted by the terms hereof, will not be disclosed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives. The Parties further agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than in connection with a possible or actual Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'will be kept confidential' creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence allows disclosure to Representatives under specific conditions, and now the second sentence states that Confidential Information 'may be disclosed' without specifying conditions. This contradicts the later part of the same sentence, which limits disclosures unless expressly permitted or consented to. The initial removal creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. It is understood that the Receiving Party may disclose any of the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party's (and its subsidiaries' and affiliates') directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys and financial advisors), and prospective bank or institutional lenders, (collectively, "Representatives") who require such material in connection with a possible or actual Transaction (provided that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall agree to act in accordance with the terms hereof). The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information of the Furnishing Party may be disclosed by the Receiving Party and its Representatives and, except with the specific prior written consent of the Furnishing Party, or as expressly otherwise permitted by the terms hereof, will not be disclosed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives. The Parties further agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than in connection with a possible or actual Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information of the Furnishing Party 'will be kept confidential'. The changed text removes this obligation, stating instead that it 'may be disclosed'. This directly contradicts the later part of the same sentence, which limits disclosures except with prior written consent or as expressly permitted, and the earlier part of the section defining to whom the information may be disclosed. The changed text, by stating information 'may be disclosed' without specifying conditions, creates an in-text contradiction according to Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall limit disclosure' to 'shall disclose' creates an immediate in-text contradiction. The paragraph initially sets up stricter rules for 'Highly Confidential Information.' Then, it states that Company *shall* disclose this information to employees, directly contradicting the subsequent sentence that prohibits disclosure to consultants, vendors, or agents. This introduces a conflict about how the company is supposed to handle the data internally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees' to 'Company shall disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees'. This directly contradicts the subsequent sentence in the same paragraph, 'Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.' The instruction to disclose to employees inherently conflicts with the restriction on disclosing to consultants/vendors/agents, which the explanation correctly identifies as a strong, in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same location because it's a direct in-text contradiction, as stated in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party may or may not deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will promptly deliver' to 'may or may not deliver', this introduces an in-text contradiction by making the return of confidential information optional. The original clause clearly obligates the Receiving Party to return the specified information. The revised version provides an option, which contradicts the expectation of mandatory return implied by the clause's initial condition of providing a list.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party may or may not deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party 'will promptly deliver' the Confidential Information, creating an obligation. The changed text alters this to 'may or may not deliver', introducing optionality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is identical to the Changed Text, because it turns a mandatory action into a discretionary one, resulting in an in-text contradiction within the contract itself. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (excluding legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity' to 'excluding legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity', the Receiving Party's obligation to fully indemnify the Furnishing Party is changed. It creates a contradiction because while the Receiving party has the right to equitable relief, they are now unable to request legal fees if said event happens. The initial statement that 'money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement' makes this contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (excluding legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity' in the Receiving Party's indemnification obligation. The changed text explicitly excludes these fees, directly contradicting the acknowledgement that 'money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement'. By removing the legal fees from the indemnity, the Furnishing Party may not be fully compensated for a breach, which conflicts with the stated insufficiency of monetary damages, thus creating an in-text contradiction as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
